description.value:
Trigger A creature enters any area of the hazard

Effect The reality warp causes the timeline to repeatedly blip and reset, approximately every six seconds, causing the triggering creature to flicker back and forth in time. They must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save. The hazard rolls initiative and one area (A1–A5) becomes active, determined randomly.

Critical Success The triggering creature uses the time blip to their advantage; on the first check they attempt on their turn each round, they can roll twice and take the better result, until the hazard is defeated. This is a fortune effect.

Success The triggering creature is unaffected.

Failure On the first check the triggering creature attempts on their turn each round, they must roll twice and take the worse result, until the hazard is defeated. This is a misfortune effect.

Critical Failure As failure, except the triggering creature must roll every check twice.

details.description: Reality glitches, causing one of the camp's five areas to jump backward and forward in time, buildings and people alike dissolving in the temporal chaos.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:18], @Check[nature|dc:18], @Check[occultism|dc:18], or @Check[religion|dc:18] (trained) to magically reinforce Akiton's reality, or @Check[intimidation|dc:19] (trained) to impose your will on the reality warp; must be in the hazard's active area to Disable it.
details.routine: (2 actions) With its first action, reality warps in the hazard's active area, dealing @Damage[(1d10+6)[force]] damage to all creatures in that area (@Check[fortitude|dc:20|basic] save). With its second action, the active hazard moves to a different area, determined randomly. Any creatures in this new active area must attempt a @Check[will|dc:20] save. On a failure, they are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Stunned]{Stunned 1} as they try to process the changing reality; on a critical failure, they are also @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.Off-Guard] for 1 round.
